content.text: Inserts HTML content immediately after the specified element
Throws if htmlString contains invalid HTML
Throws if elem is null or not an HTMLElement
- Sanitize HTML content to prevent XSS

summary.text: The reference element to insert content after

text.comment: The reference element to insert content after

summary.text: The HTML string to insert (must be valid HTML)

text.comment: The HTML string to insert (must be valid HTML)

Description:

Inserts HTML content immediately after the specified element

Returns:
Throws:

Throws if htmlString contains invalid HTML

Throws:

Throws if elem is null or not an HTMLElement

Example:
// Insert a simple element after another
const target = document.getElementById('target');
insertAfter(target, '<div class="notification">Content added!</div>');
Example:
// Insert complex HTML structure
const listItem = document.querySelector('li:last-child');
const newItemHTML = `
  <li role="listitem">
    <span>New Item</span>
    <button aria-label="Remove item">×</button>
  </li>
`;
insertAfter(listItem, newItemHTML);
Example:
// Security consideration - sanitize user input
const userContent = escapeHTML(userInput);
const safeHTML = `<div class="user-content">${userContent}</div>`;
insertAfter(targetElement, safeHTML);
Since:

1.0.0

See:
  • insertBefore - Insert content before an element
  • escapeHTML - Sanitize HTML content to prevent XSS
  • https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/insertAdjacentHTML - Browser support: IE 4+, all browsers
  • https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/parsing.html - WCAG: Ensure valid HTML markup
  • https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Cross_Site_Scripting_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html - XSS prevention guidelines
